caption: You worked 40 hours at $15/hr. That's $600. Your check says $487. That's not a mistake — that's federal tax, FICA, and maybe state tax doing their thing before you ever see it. Know the difference between gross and net before you budget a single dollar.

hook_lines.text: compound interest doesn't care about your excuse.

caption: Waiting for the 'right amount' to start saving is the #1 excuse that costs you the most. $10/week at 18 becomes real money by 30 — not because $10 is magic, but because starting now means time does the heavy lifting. Open the account before you have a reason to.

Forget the vague 'start a side hustle' advice. Dog walking: $15-20/walk. Lawn care: $30-50/yard. Tutoring: $20-40/hr. Selling on Depop: 70-90% of listed price after fees. Pick one, start this week, and track every dollar — that's how you build your first real savings.

hook_lines.text: habit 2: they save before they spend.

caption: The difference between people who build wealth and people who don't isn't income — it's habits. Track every dollar for one month. Save the first 10% the second you get paid, not what's 'left over.' Know your net worth number, even if it's small. Small habits compound into big gaps.

hook_lines.text: this is my actual budget spreadsheet.
not a template. what I actually spend.
steal this format — link's on my page.

caption: Here's my real budget, categories and all — rent, food, subscriptions, savings, the works. I'm not telling you to copy my numbers, I'm showing you the structure so you can build your own. Screenshot this, make your own version, and actually use it.

hook_lines.text: this is my actual roth ira breakdown.

caption: Here's my actual Roth IRA — the funds, the percentages, all of it. I started with $50 because that's what I had, not because it was the 'right' amount. A real portfolio beats a hypothetical one every time. Full breakdown is on my page if you want to build your own.
